name: AME
description: Aviation Medical Examiner: FAA-designated physician who performs pilot medical examinations.
med_cert_importance: Conducts medical examinations and issues medical certificates for pilots.

name: SI
description: Issuance of a certificate to fly with specific medical conditions.
med_cert_importance: Allows pilots with disqualifying conditions to obtain certification with restrictions or under specific conditions.

name: BasicMed
description: Alternative medical certification for certain operations.
med_cert_importance: Simplifies medical certification using personal physician instead of AME.

name: Aerospace medical dispositions
description: These are FAA-defined outcomes or decisions that guide how specific medical conditions should be evaluated. They outline what an examiner should do when a condition is found.
med_cert_importance: They determine whether a pilot can be issued a medical certificate, requires more testing, needs a special issuance, or must be deferred.

name: Protocols
description: Protocols are standardized procedures that guide how medical evaluations should be performed. They ensure pilots are assessed consistently.
med_cert_importance: They help ensure medical exams follow FAA-approved methods so every applicant is judged under uniform safety standards.

name: AME Assisted Special Issuance (AASI)
description: A process allowing an AME to renew a medical certificate for pilots with certain conditions after the FAA initially grants a Special Issuance. It simplifies renewals for stable conditions.
med_cert_importance: It allows pilots with long-term but stable medical issues to continue flying without needing a full FAA review every time.

name: FAA Form 8500
description: The official application form that pilots complete before undergoing an FAA medical exam. It includes medical history questions used in certification decisions.
med_cert_importance: It forms the basis of the pilot's medical record and determines what conditions or testing must be reviewed.

name: Eligibility for medical certification
description: A pilot's ability to meet FAA medical standards needed to safely operate an aircraft. Eligibility depends on health history, exam findings, and FAA requirements.
med_cert_importance: Determines whether a pilot can legally fly. Failing to meet requirements may lead to deferral, denial, or a special issuance.

name: Authorization of Special Issuance
description: An FAA authorization that allows a pilot with an otherwise disqualifying medical condition to fly if they demonstrate adequate safety and control of the condition.
med_cert_importance: It enables pilots with significant but stable or well-controlled medical issues to maintain certification under strict monitoring.

name: Statement of Demonstrated Ability (SODA)
description: A document issued by the FAA when a pilot has specific stable, non-progressive, medical conditions that does not worsen over time. It serves as long-term approval.
med_cert_importance: Allows pilots to avoid repeated reevaluation for certain stable conditions and continue flying without new special issuances.

name: Deferral
description: When an AME sends a medical application to the FAA for further review instead of issuing or denying it. This happens when more information is required.
med_cert_importance: It prevents incorrect or unsafe decisions by involving FAA experts when a medical issue is unclear or borderline.

name: Denial
description: A formal FAA decision that an applicant does not meet the medical standards required to hold a pilot certificate.
med_cert_importance: Stops a pilot from exercising flying privileges and requires significant documentation or recovery before reapplying.
